head.title: Somnath Dwarka Tour From Pune, Flights Included
head.meta_description: Pune flies direct to central Rajkot, so skip the Mumbai connection. Flights in the price. Ground from Rs.15,000 pp, all-in about Rs.22,000.
head.h1: Somnath Dwarka Tour Package From Pune, With Flights Included
head.og_title: Somnath Dwarka From Pune: Fly Direct to Rajkot, Skip Mumbai
head.og_description: Pune has a non-stop to central Rajkot from around Rs. 3,300. Flights in the price. The cheapest fly-in on the site.

copy.quick_answer: A Somnath Dwarka tour package from Pune has a simpler answer than most Pune travellers expect, because the reflex is to route everything through Mumbai and here you should not. Pune flies non-stop to Rajkot Hirasar on IndiGo from around Rs. 3,300, and Rajkot is the most central airport for both temples. So you fly direct into the middle of the circuit, we meet you at Hirasar, and the trip starts without a Mumbai connection. The ground portion is fixed at Rs. 5,000 per person per night, so four days is Rs. 15,000 per person at 3 star with hotel, breakfast and vehicle, and with the cheap return flight added the all-in is typically around Rs. 22,000 per person, the lowest fly-in on the site. Mumbai is worth the detour only if you want to land at Dwarka's doorstep, since it holds the only non-stop to Jamnagar.

sections.h2: The Pune reflex, and why it is wrong here
body: Pune travellers reach for Mumbai. It is three hours away, it flies everywhere, and for most destinations that Pune does not serve directly the Mumbai connection is the sensible move.

For this circuit it is not. Pune has its own non-stop to Rajkot Hirasar, on IndiGo, from around Rs. 3,300, and Rajkot is the most central airport for both temples. Adding a Mumbai leg buys you nothing but a longer day and a second fare.
So the Pune answer is the direct one. Fly Pune to Rajkot, land in the middle of the circuit, and start.
There is one honest exception, and it is below.

sections.h2: Fly direct to Rajkot, the airport in the middle
body: Pune to Rajkot Hirasar is a non-stop on IndiGo, and it is one of the cheaper ways into this circuit from anywhere, with fares that have started around Rs. 3,300. Hirasar sits about 30 km outside Rajkot.

Rajkot is the most balanced airport on the whole route. It is 197 km from Somnath and 225 to 235 km from Dwarka, so both temples are a half day's drive and neither is stranded far away. From Rajkot, Somnath is the nearer of the two.

We track the flight, meet you at Hirasar and start the ground trip the same afternoon. Because Somnath is closer from Rajkot, a Pune arrival often runs the circuit Somnath first, and we set the order to your flight time and dates.

sections.h2: The one time Mumbai is worth the detour
body: If landing as close to Dwarka as possible matters more to you than a clean single flight, Mumbai is the reason to route through it.

Mumbai, three hours from Pune by road or a short hop by air, has the only non-stop to Jamnagar, the airport nearest Dwarka, about 90 minutes on IndiGo. From Jamnagar it is roughly two hours to the Dwarkadhish temple.

So a Dwarka-first pilgrim can go Pune to Mumbai to Jamnagar and begin at Dwarka's doorstep. It adds a leg and a fare against the direct Rajkot flight, and we lay that tradeoff out plainly before you choose. For most people the direct Rajkot flight wins.

sections.h2: What all inclusive means here, and what it costs
body: This is one of the two package classes on the site with the flight in the price, and it is the cheapest of them because the Pune fare is low. We book the return flight, the hotels, the breakfast and the vehicle with driver for the full circuit.

The ground portion is fixed. Four days and three nights is Rs. 15,000 per person at the 3 star tier, Rs. 30,000 at 4 star and Rs. 37,500 at 5 star, GST included, with hotel, breakfast, vehicle, tolls, parking, fuel and the driver's food and stay all inside it.

The flight is the part that moves. Pune to Rajkot non-stop has started around Rs. 3,300 each way and changes with the date and the booking window, so a four day all-in at 3 star typically lands around Rs. 22,000 per person. We quote the exact combined figure for your dates.
Book earlier and the flight half falls further. On Janmashtami and Diwali both halves rise, the ground rate up to threefold and airfares with it, so those dates are booked about three months ahead.

itinerary.note: The four day plan from Pune, shown at its two ends. Day one is the direct flight in and the first darshan, the final day is the flight home, and the shared circuit days are handed to the four day plan that owns them.

days.title: Pune to Rajkot direct, then into the circuit

stops.text: Non-stop Pune to Rajkot Hirasar on IndiGo. No Mumbai connection. We meet you in arrivals at Hirasar, about 30 km outside Rajkot.
Drive begins. Because Somnath is the nearer temple from Rajkot at 197 km, a Rajkot arrival often runs Somnath first, and we set the exact order to your flight time.
Reach the first night's town, check in at your tier, and take the darshan or aarti the arrival time allows.
Night one on the circuit, with the long Gujarat drive and the Mumbai layover both avoided.

days.title: The middle of the trip, shared by every start city
days.intro: Once you are on the circuit your start city stops mattering, so rather than reprint the same two days on every origin page we hand them to the plan that owns them.
days.summary: Two full days of Dwarka, Bet Dwarka across Sudarshan Setu, Nageshwar and the coastal run to Somnath through Porbandar, set out hour by hour on the four day plan.
days.handoff: The full hour by hour version of both days sits on the four day plan. It is the same middle you are buying here. See /somnath-dwarka-tour-package/4-days-3-nights/

days.title: Back to Rajkot, and the flight home to Pune

stops.text: The last darshan, then the drive back toward Hirasar, timed to your flight.
Drop at Rajkot Hirasar for the non-stop back to Pune, again with no connection.
Home in Pune the same day, no overnight travel either way.

price_sheet.intro: Two parts, one quote. The ground portion is fixed per person per night. The return flight is added at the live fare, and because the Pune fare is low, the combined number is the lowest of the fly-in cities.

table.hotels: The Fern Sattva Resort at Dwarka, best property in town at Somnath

price_sheet.all_in_note: All in note. The all-in figures assume a return Pune to Rajkot non-stop from around Rs. 3,300 each way. Airfare moves with the date and the booking window, so we quote the exact combined number for your dates rather than hold you to an estimate.
price_sheet.gst_note: Gst note. Every ground price here is final and includes GST. Nothing is added at checkout beyond the flight, which is shown to you at its actual fare.
price_sheet.group_and_child: Group and child. The ground rate is per person on twin sharing. In a group of about six the 3 star ground rate falls to around Rs. 4,000 per person per night on triple sharing, and to around Rs. 3,500 in a group of about twelve. A solo traveller pays the base plus Rs. 3,500 for the same services. Children under five travel free on the ground portion, and from five they are counted as an adult. Airfare is per seat at whatever the airline charges for the age.
price_sheet.vehicle_note: Vehicle note. Group size picks the vehicle. Sedan for 2 to 3, Ertiga for 4 to 5, Innova Crysta for 5 to 6, 9 seater Tempo Traveller for 7 to 9, 12 seater Maharaja or Urbania for 10 to 12, 26 seater Tempo Traveller above that.
price_sheet.included_note: Included note. The package covers the return Pune to Rajkot flight, the hotel at your tier, breakfast, the vehicle with driver for the full circuit, and all road tolls, parking, fuel and the driver's food and stay. You land to a car and a plan.
price_sheet.five_star_honesty: Five star honesty. No five star Taj or ITC property operates in Dwarka or in Somnath. In Dwarka the top tier puts you in The Fern Sattva Resort. At Somnath it buys the best room in town, named in your quote before you pay. Taj and ITC enter your plan only on the legs that reach them, which this circuit does not.
price_sheet.blackout_note: Blackout note. Two dates break this rate card. On Janmashtami and Diwali, ground demand can push prices up to three times these numbers and airfares climb with them, and we take a limited number of bookings made about three months ahead. If your travel lands on either, ask early and we will quote the real figure rather than a floor you cannot get.
price_sheet.exclusions: Lunch and dinner, personal pooja, seva and abhishek at either temple, the sound and light show ticket, camera and cloakroom charges, tips, in-flight meals, seat selection and excess baggage charged by the airline, and anything you buy yourself. The Gir safari permit is extra if you add Gir.
price_sheet.cancellation: Cancellation. We confirm a booking on 50 percent advance, and you pay the remaining 50 percent after you arrive, during the journey. The flight follows the airline's own fare rules, which we tell you before you pay. Cancellation, refunds and the payment schedule for the ground portion are set out in full on our cancellation and refund policy, and the booking terms sit alongside it. Read both before you pay.
price_sheet.trust_warning: Trust warning. The Shree Somnath Trust accepts pooja and donation bookings only through its own website and warns that it never asks for payment through private numbers, WhatsApp or UPI. Book sevas yourself at somnath.org.

why_choose: We tell Pune travellers what their booking habit hides: Pune flies direct to central Rajkot, so the Mumbai connection is not needed here.
The cheapest fly-in on the site, because the Pune to Rajkot fare is low and we pass the real fare through rather than pad it.
One price, one booking. The return flight, the hotels, the breakfast and the car are all ours to arrange.
We name the one case where Mumbai is worth the detour, the Jamnagar non-stop for a Dwarka-first arrival, rather than pretend the direct flight suits everyone.
A local Gujarat team meets your flight and drives the circuit. One number, +91 7302265809, on call and WhatsApp.
We reconfirm every timing and the flight before departure, because festival schedules and fares both move at Shravan, Janmashtami and Mahashivratri.
copy.not_for_you_if_heading: This trip is not for you if
not_for_you_if: You only want the tour and will book your own flight. Then the Rajkot land page is the honest fit, and this flight-inclusive page is more than you need.
You are set on flying into Jamnagar without any stop. There is no non-stop from Pune to Jamnagar; the nearest airport to Dwarka is reachable from Pune only through Mumbai or another connection.
You want one fixed all-in price months out regardless of the airfare. The ground half is fixed, the flight half moves, and an honest all-in is quoted per date.
You want to fly in and see both temples in a day. From any Gujarat airport that is a rushed blur. Give it four days.
You want a five star Taj in Dwarka. It does not exist, and anyone selling you one should be asked for the property name.

faq.q: Should I fly from Pune or go via Mumbai for Dwarka and Somnath?
faq.a: Fly from Pune. Pune has a non-stop to Rajkot Hirasar, the most central airport for both temples, from around Rs. 3,300. Routing through Mumbai adds a leg and a fare for no gain, unless you specifically want to land at Dwarka's doorstep, in which case Mumbai's non-stop to Jamnagar is the reason to detour.

faq.q: Is there a direct flight from Pune to Dwarka or Jamnagar?
faq.a: There is no airport at Dwarka, and Pune has no non-stop to Jamnagar, the airport nearest it. Pune's non-stop into the circuit is to Rajkot Hirasar, about 225 km from Dwarka and 197 km from Somnath, which is why we route this package through Rajkot.

faq.q: Which airport is nearest to Dwarka and Somnath?
faq.a: Jamnagar is nearest to Dwarka at about two hours. For both temples together, Rajkot is the most balanced at 197 km from Somnath and 225 km from Dwarka, and from Pune it has a cheap non-stop, so it is the airport this package flies into.

faq.q: How much is a Dwarka Somnath package from Pune with flights?
faq.a: The ground portion is fixed at Rs. 15,000 per person for four days at 3 star, GST included. With a return Pune to Rajkot flight from around Rs. 3,300 each way, the all-in is typically around Rs. 22,000 per person, the lowest of the fly-in cities, and we quote the exact figure for your dates.

faq.q: How many days do you need for Dwarka and Somnath from Pune?
faq.a: Four days and three nights. Flying direct saves the arrival drive, but both temples, the island, the bridge and the coast still need three nights to see without rushing. Two nights works if your flight times are kind.

final_cta.summary: The cheapest fly-in on the site, and the one that corrects a Pune habit: fly direct to central Rajkot, do not route through Mumbai. Ground fixed at Rs. 15,000 per person at 3 star, all-in typically around Rs. 22,000, quoted for your dates. Mumbai only if you want Dwarka's doorstep.
final_cta.primary: Get an all-in quote for your dates

quotable_first_party_claims: Somnath Dwarka from Pune, ground fixed at Rs. 5,000 per person per night, Rs. 15,000 per person for four days at 3 star, and with a return flight added the all-in is typically around Rs. 22,000, the lowest fly-in on the site.
Pune flies non-stop to Rajkot Hirasar on IndiGo from around Rs. 3,300, the most central airport for both temples, so from Pune you should not route through Mumbai.
Mumbai is worth the detour from Pune only if you want to land at Dwarka's doorstep, since it holds the only non-stop to Jamnagar.
